title: Fix a Loose Handle
summary: Tighten or adjust a wobbly door handle, cabinet knob, or drawer pull so it feels solid and works smoothly.
learnerPromise: Fix a wobbly door handle, cabinet knob, or drawer pull.
whyItMatters: A loose handle can be frustrating to use and may eventually fall off. Fixing it early prevents damage and makes daily tasks easier.
realLifeUses: Tightening a loose bathroom door handle
Fixing a kitchen cabinet knob that spins
Securing a drawer pull that wiggles
Helping a friend or family member with a quick repair
youWillLearn: How to find the screw or fastener on a handle
Which tool to use for most common handles
How to tighten without stripping the screw
When a handle may need replacement instead
miniChallenge: Check one handle or knob in your home and tighten or adjust it.
steps: Find the loose handle, knob, or pull you want to fix.
Look for screws on the handle or on the plate behind it.
Choose the right screwdriver or tool that fits the screw head.
Turn the screw clockwise until it feels snug. Do not overtighten.
Test the handle to make sure it feels solid and moves smoothly.
completionCriteria: You identified the screw or fastener on the handle
You tightened it securely without stripping
The handle no longer wobbles or spins
commonProblems: The screw keeps spinning and will not tighten — the threads may be stripped
The handle still wobbles after tightening — check for a second screw
You do not have the right tool — ask to borrow one or check a basic toolkit
tips: If the screw is stripped, removing it and adding a small piece of toothpick can help
Cabinet knobs usually have one screw from the inside of the door
Door handles often have two screws on the base plate
helpBoundary: Ask for help if the fixture is cracked or you need power tools.
